title: Per-topic misinformation hot-spot map (unproven cures, outdated survival stats) from vetted sources
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. This task maps, per topic and strictly from vetted sources, the common misinformation hot-spots (unproven cures, outdated survival statistics) so later drafting can pre-empt them — without itself giving advice or prognosis. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Produce a per-topic misinformation hot-spot map (e.g. unproven/commercial cures, outdated survival statistics), grounded only in vetted sources, to guide misinformation-correction in later content.
acceptanceCriteria: Identifies, per topic, common misinformation hot-spots (unproven/commercial cures, outdated or misread survival statistics) families encounter
Each correction is grounded in a vetted source (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG) with provenance; no model-generated medical facts
Frames corrections as general education with survival-stats framing ('groups, not your child/you'); gives no individual prognosis or advice
